A lot of people talk about hiring great talent

Far fewer talk about building the environment where that talent actually does its best work

- Creative freedom
- Ownership
- Systems that evolve with feedback
- Room to experiment
- Safety to make mistakes
- A chance to learn from

If you spent half the time you spend doomscrolling on X or Instagram on Pinterest, I bet you’d be 10x more creative 

(Doesn’t matter what you do for a living)

bear market musings...

building in a bear market is scary. you don’t know if users will come, and most things you try probably won’t work.

but that also gives builders room to experiment, figure out what works, and be ready when the market picks up again. you can’t get

risk-to-earn games could work even better as their own chain

their fast, high-RTP loops naturally drive a lot of onchain activity:

-> pay to enter a round and win from a shared prize pool
-> settle quickly, then play again
-> grind and buy items to improve odds or RTP
